NCT03634982.title: Dose Escalation of RMC-4630 Monotherapy in Relapsed/Refractory Solid Tumors

eligibility.text: Participant (male or female) ≥18 years of age
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures a sufficient sample size and addresses potential recruitment challenges.

eligibility.text: Participants who have advanced solid tumors that have failed, are intolerant to, or are considered ineligible for standard of care anticancer treatments including approved drugs for oncogenic drivers in their tumor type
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the study population is representative of patients with a specific condition and maximizes the likelihood of detecting treatment effects.

eligibility.text: Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group (ECOG) performance status of 0 to 1
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures participants are able to tolerate the study's demands and minimize potential complications.

eligibility.text: Participants in the Dose-Expansion Component must have one of the following genotypic aberrations: KRAS amplifications, KRASG12C (NSCLC), BRAF Class 3, or NF1 LOF (NSCLC and gynecological cancers) mutations
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures participants are eligible for the dose-expansion component based on specific genetic markers.

eligibility.text: Adequate hematologic, hepatic and renal function
eligibility.justification: This criterion protects patients from potential adverse effects of the study treatment.

eligibility.text: Participant able to understand and voluntarily sign the informed consent form (ICF) and able to comply with the study visit schedule and other protocol requirements.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures participant comprehension and compliance, which are essential for successful trial participation.

eligibility.text: Participants willing to agree to not father a child/become pregnant and comply with effective contraception criteria
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the study population is free of potential pregnancy complications.

eligibility.text: Known or suspected leptomeningeal or brain metastases or spinal cord compression
eligibility.justification: This criterion excludes patients with conditions that could be exacerbated by the study treatment.

eligibility.text: Primary central nervous system (CNS) tumors
eligibility.justification: This criterion excludes patients with CNS tumors, which may be sensitive to the study treatment.

eligibility.text: Clinically significant cardiac disease
eligibility.justification: This criterion excludes patients with pre-existing cardiovascular conditions that could be exacerbated by the study treatment.

eligibility.text: Active, clinically significant interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis
eligibility.justification: This criterion excludes patients with lung diseases that may be sensitive to the study treatment.

eligibility.text: History or current evidence of retinal pigment epithelial detachment (RPED), central serous retinopathy, retinal vein occlusion (RVO), or predisposing factors to RPED or RVO
eligibility.justification: This criterion excludes patients with eye conditions that could be exacerbated by the study treatment.

eligibility.text: Known HIV infection
eligibility.justification: This criterion excludes patients with HIV, which may be sensitive to the study treatment.

eligibility.text: Active/chronic hepatitis B or C infection
eligibility.justification: This criterion excludes patients with active or chronic liver infections that could be exacerbated by the study treatment.

eligibility.text: Any other unstable or clinically significant concurrent medical condition that would, in the opinion of the investigator, jeopardize the safety of a participant, impact their expected survival through the end of the study participation, and/or impact their ability to comply with the protocol prior/concomitant therapy
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patient safety by excluding those with conditions that could be exacerbated or negatively impacted by the study treatment.

eligibility.text: Females who are pregnant or breastfeeding
eligibility.justification: This criterion excludes women of childbearing potential to prevent pregnancy complications and ensure participant safety.

power_analysis.assessment: N/A — Phase 1 trial, not powered for efficacy

endpoints.text: Number of participants with adverse events (AEs)
Number of participants with dose limiting toxicities (DLTs)

adaptive_designs.description: Adaptive design detected: Types identified: Dose-Finding / Phase I.

safety_adverse_events.ae_ascertainment: Not explicitly described (assumed standard investigator reporting)
safety_adverse_events.safety_monitoring: Investigator-reported with sponsor oversight (no DSMB mentioned)

pharmacogenetics.genetic_screen_failure_summary: Detected 1 genetic biomarker requirement(s) in eligibility: KRAS_G12C (pop. prevalence ~13%, ~87% screen fail). Worst-case screen failure rate from genetic criteria: ~87%.
pharmacogenetics.summary: Genes detected (PTPN11) but could not map indication "relapsed/refractory solid tumors" to a GWAS EFO trait. Try alternative trait name.

relaxations.description: Decrease minimum age from 18 to 13 years
relaxations.raw_text: * Participant (male or female) ≥18 years of age

NCT03989115.title: Dose-Esc/Exp RMC4630 & Cobi in Relapsed/Refractory Solid Tumors & RMC4630& Osi in EGFR+ Locally Adv/Meta NSCLC

eligibility.text: Age ≥18 years
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures eligibility for the trial and is a standard inclusion criteria.

eligibility.text: For RMC-4630 + Cobimetinib only - Participants who have advanced solid tumors that have failed, are intolerant to, or are considered ineligible for standard of care anti-cancer treatments including approved drugs for oncogenic drivers in their tumor type.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the patient is eligible for treatment and aligns with safety concerns regarding treatment eligibility.

eligibility.text: For RMC-4630 + Osimertinib only - Locally advanced or metastatic EGFR mutant NSCLC not amenable to curative surgery or radiotherapy
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the patient is eligible for treatment and aligns with statistical power concerns regarding disease severity.

eligibility.text: For RMC-4630 + Cobimetinib only - Participants must have one of the following genotypic aberrations: KRAS mutations and amplifications, BRAF Class 3 mutations, or NF1 LOF mutations
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the patient is eligible for treatment and aligns with statistical power concerns regarding disease severity.

eligibility.text: For RMC-4630 + Osimertinib only - Evidence of radiological documentation of progression with osimertinib monotherapy or an osimertinib containing regimen. Participants should not be considered a current candidate for 1st generation EGFR TKI's by the investigator.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the patient is eligible for treatment and aligns with statistical power concerns regarding disease progression.

eligibility.text: Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group (ECOG) performance status of ≤1
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the patient is eligible for treatment and aligns with safety concerns regarding patient health.

eligibility.text: Life expectancy >12 weeks
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the patient is eligible for treatment and aligns with safety concerns regarding patient health.

eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the patient is eligible for treatment and aligns with feasibility concerns regarding pregnancy.

eligibility.text: Primary central nervous system (CNS) tumors.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the patient is eligible for treatment and aligns with safety concerns regarding CNS health.

eligibility.text: Known or suspected leptomeningeal or brain metastases or spinal cord compression.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the patient is eligible for treatment and aligns with safety concerns regarding neurological health.

eligibility.text: For RMC-4630 + osimertinib arm only - Known or suspected Small cell, squamous, or pleomorphic lung transformations
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the patient is eligible for treatment and aligns with safety concerns regarding lung health.

eligibility.text: Clinically significant cardiac disease
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the patient is eligible for treatment and aligns with safety concerns regarding cardiovascular health.

eligibility.text: Active, clinically signiﬁcant interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the patient is eligible for treatment and aligns with safety concerns regarding respiratory health.

eligibility.text: History or current evidence of retinal pigment epithelial detachment (RPED), central serous retinopathy, retinal vein occlusion, or predisposing factors to RPED or RVO
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the patient is eligible for treatment and aligns with safety concerns regarding eye health.

eligibility.text: Known HIV infection or active/chronic hepatitis B or C infection.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the patient is eligible for treatment and aligns with safety concerns regarding immune system health.

eligibility.text: Any other unstable or clinically signiﬁcant concurrent medical condition that would, in the opinion of the investigator, jeopardize the safety of a participant, impact their expected survival through the end of the study participation, and/or impact their ability to comply with the protocol prior/concomitant therapy
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the patient is eligible for treatment and aligns with safety concerns regarding overall health.

eligibility.text: Females who are pregnant or breastfeeding
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the patient is eligible for treatment and aligns with safety concerns regarding pregnancy and breastfeeding.

eligibility.text: Subject must be ≥18 years of age.
eligibility.justification: Age is a standard feasibility criterion for clinical trials.

eligibility.text: Subject must have pathologically documented, locally advanced or metastatic KRASG12C NSCLC (not amenable to curative surgery) that has progressed on prior standard therapies (no more than 3 prior lines of therapies are allowed)
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the study population is representative of a specific patient group with a known treatment challenge.

eligibility.text: Exclusion Criteria
eligibility.justification: The text itself indicates that this is an exclusion criteria, not a criterion for inclusion.

eligibility.text: Primary central nervous system (CNS) tumors
eligibility.justification: This criterion protects the patient from potential harm by excluding those with CNS tumors.

eligibility.text: Known or suspected leptomeningeal or brain metastases or spinal cord compression
eligibility.justification: This criterion prioritizes patient safety by excluding individuals at risk of complications from these conditions.

eligibility.text: Clinically significant cardiac disease
eligibility.justification: This criterion protects patients from potential harm by excluding those with pre-existing cardiac issues.

eligibility.text: Known impairment of GI function that would alter the absorption
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patient compliance and data quality, as it relates to medication absorption.

eligibility.text: Active autoimmune disease requiring systemic treatment within past 2 years
eligibility.justification: This criterion protects patients from potential harm by excluding those with active autoimmune diseases that may interfere with the study intervention.

eligibility.text: History of severe allergic reactions to any of the study intervention components
eligibility.justification: This criterion prioritizes patient safety by excluding individuals with a history of severe allergic reactions to potential study interventions.

eligibility.text: Major surgical procedures within 28 days or non-study-related minor procedures within 7 days of treatment.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patient safety and data quality by excluding individuals who may be at risk for complications from surgery or other medical interventions.

eligibility.text: Prior therapy with KRASG12C inhibitor and/or SHP2 inhibitor
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the study population is representative of a specific patient group with a known treatment challenge.

NCT06445062.title: Study of RAS(ON) Inhibitors in Patients With Gastrointestinal Solid Tumors

eligibility.text: ≥ 18 years of age
eligibility.justification: Age is a standard feasibility criterion for clinical trials.

eligibility.text: ECOG PS is 0 to 1
eligibility.justification: The ECOG performance status score helps assess the likelihood of patients responding to treatment, thus impacting statistical power.

eligibility.text: Adequate organ function as outlined by the study
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patient safety and minimizes potential complications from treatment.

eligibility.text: Pathologically or cytologically documented pancreatic carcinoma or poorly differentiated pancreatic carcinoma with metastatic disease or RAS-mutated, histologically or cytologically confirmed colorectal adenocarcinoma with documented unresectable or metastatic disease (Subprotocol A, B, and C)
eligibility.justification: This criterion is a safety measure to exclude patients with specific cancers.

eligibility.text: Presence of RAS G12D mutation (Subprotocol D, E, F)
eligibility.justification: The presence of the RAS G12D mutation is a factor that influences treatment efficacy and thus impacts statistical power.

eligibility.text: Primary central nervous system (CNS) tumors
eligibility.justification: This criterion excludes patients with CNS tumors, which can be associated with increased risk of complications or treatment failure.

eligibility.text: Impaired gastrointestinal (GI) function that may significantly alter the absorption of RMC drugs
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients can tolerate the drug and its potential side effects, impacting feasibility.

eligibility.text: Major surgery within 28 days of first dose
eligibility.justification: This criterion is a safety measure to prevent complications from surgery and treatment.

eligibility.text: Other inclusion/exclusion criteria may apply.
eligibility.justification: This is a general feasibility criterion, as the specific criteria will vary based on the study design.

power_analysis.assessment: N/A — Phase 1 trial, not powered for efficacy

adaptive_designs.description: Adaptive design detected: Types identified: Dose-Finding / Phase I.

safety_adverse_events.ae_ascertainment: Not explicitly described (assumed standard investigator reporting)
safety_adverse_events.safety_monitoring: Investigator-reported with sponsor oversight (no DSMB mentioned)

known_effects: Treatment response - KRAS G12D mutation required

pharmacogenetics.genetic_screen_failure_summary: Detected 1 genetic biomarker requirement(s) in eligibility: KRAS_G12D (pop. prevalence ~5%, ~95% screen fail). Worst-case screen failure rate from genetic criteria: ~95%.

known_effects: Treatment response - KRAS G12D mutation required

pharmacogenetics.stratification_opportunity: Yes — genotype-stratified randomization or pre-planned subgroup analysis warranted. Per textbook Ch. 9: "one would in subgroup analysis compare treatment responses such as serious adverse events."
pharmacogenetics.summary: Found 0 GWAS association(s) relevant to 3 pharmacogenetic subgroup(s) in family history of upper gastrointestinal cancer. 1 genetic screen requirement(s) detected. Stratification opportunity identified.

NCT06625320.title: Phase 3 Study of Daraxonrasib (RMC-6236) in Patients With Previously Treated Metastatic Pancreatic Ductal Adenocarcinoma (PDAC)

eligibility.text: At least 18 years old and has provided informed consent.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures compliance with legal requirements for clinical trials involving human subjects.

eligibility.text: Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group (ECOG) performance status of 0 or 1.
eligibility.justification: This criterion is a safety measure to ensure patients are physically capable of participating in the trial.

eligibility.text: Histologically or cytologically confirmed PDAC with metastatic disease.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures that participants have a specific diagnosis and disease stage, which is relevant for assessing treatment efficacy.

eligibility.text: Measurable disease per RECIST 1.1.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the study can detect a treatment effect by measuring tumor size and response to therapy.

eligibility.text: Adequate organ function (bone marrow, liver, kidney, coagulation)
eligibility.justification: This criterion is a safety measure to ensure patients have sufficient organ function to tolerate the study treatment.

eligibility.text: Documented RAS mutation status, either mutant or wild-type. RAS mutations defined as nonsynonymous mutations in KRAS, NRAS, or HRAS at codons 12, 13, or 61 (G12, G13, or Q61).
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the study population is homogenous and allows for comparison of treatment effects based on RAS mutation status.

eligibility.text: Able to take oral medications.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients can effectively receive the study treatment.

eligibility.text: Prior therapy with direct RAS-targeted therapy (eg. degraders and/or inhibitors).
eligibility.justification: This criterion is a safety measure to ensure patients have not received prior treatment that could interfere with the study treatment.

eligibility.text: History of or known central nervous system metastatic disease.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients are free from conditions that may affect their ability to participate in the trial.

eligibility.text: Any conditions that may affect the ability to take or absorb study treatment
eligibility.justification: This criterion is a feasibility measure to ensure patients can adhere to the study protocol and receive the study treatment.

eligibility.text: Major surgery within 4 weeks prior to randomization.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures that patients have sufficient recovery time before starting the trial.

eligibility.text: Patient is unable or unwilling to comply with protocol-required study visits or procedures.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patient compliance and adherence to the study protocol.

endpoints.text: Progression free survival (PFS) in the RAS G12-mutant population
Overall survival (OS) in the RAS G12-mutant population
Objective response in the RAS G12 and all-patient populations

Time to deterioration (TTD) in European Organization for Research and Treatment of Cancer Quality of Life Questionnaire Pancreatic Cancer Module (EORTC QLQ-PAN26) in the RAS G12 and all-patient populations
Time to deterioration (TTD) in European Organization for Research and Treatment of Cancer Quality of Life Questionnaire Core 30 (EORTC QLQ-C30) in the RAS G12 and all-patient populations
Objective response per investigator in RAS G12 and all- patient populations
Duration of response (DOR) in RAS G12 and all-patient populations
Time to response (TTR) in RAS G12 and all-patient populations
Percentage of patients with adverse events (AEs)
Pharmacokinetics of RMC-6236 in RAS G12 and all-patient populations

adaptive_designs.description: Standard fixed-design trial. No adaptive features detected.

safety_endpoints: Percentage of patients with adverse events (AEs)
safety_adverse_events.safety_monitoring: Investigator-reported with sponsor oversight (no DSMB mentioned)
